refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including terrain representation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR combined multiple obligations: terrain, obstacles, and work-zone representation. To ensure atomicity and clarity, these are separated into distinct sub-FRs. This refined FR focuses solely on terrain representation, avoiding any implementation details and maintaining solution neutrality.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that the system can generate and provide a representation of the terrain within the worksite area, independent of obstacle or work-zone data.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including terrain representation.
The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including obstacle representation.
The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including work-zone representation.

candidate_dps: Generate a digital terrain model representing the physical features of the worksite
Create a semantic map annotating terrain with relevant site features and safety zones
Develop a modular worksite model combining terrain data with operational policy overlays
Produce an abstracted spatial representation supporting dynamic updates and integration with planning modules
selected_dp.selected_dp: Develop a modular worksite model combining terrain data with operational policy overlays

potential_sources: Integration of terrain data and operational policies may create dependencies if not modularized
coupling_check.notes: By modularizing terrain representation and policy overlays as separate but interfaced components within the worksite model, independence is maintained and coupling minimized.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of accurate terrain data and site feature definitions
Risk that operational policies may change frequently requiring flexible model updates
Assumes planning and execution functions can consume the provided model format
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Clarify the need for modular separation between terrain representation and policy overlays to ensure maintainability and support independent updates.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for integrating and modeling worksite data including terrain and operational policies

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives terrain and sensor data from vehicles and other sources

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends and receives operational policy overlays and task assignments

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communicates with external systems and modules for data exchange

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as the DP involves data modeling and integration, which fits well within NGHE Solution's scope. Minimal diffusion as most data processing is internal.
Low containment since vehicles primarily execute tasks rather than model worksite data. High diffusion as data would need to be shared externally.
Moderate containment as teleoperation may use some operational policies but does not primarily model terrain data. Moderate diffusion expected.
Poor containment; safety services focus on alerts and safety monitoring, not data modeling. High diffusion expected.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for implementing the modular worksite model combining terrain data with operational policy overlays due to its role in data integration and modeling, resulting in high cohesion and low diffusion.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with Teleoperation if operational policies are tightly coupled with teleoperation control logic.
Dependency on timely and accurate telemetry data which may affect model accuracy and update frequency.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP logically addresses the FR by specifying a modular worksite model that integrates terrain data with operational policy overlays, directly supporting the requirement for worksite area analysis including terrain representation.

diffusion_risks: Potential overlap with Teleoperation if operational policies are tightly coupled with teleoperation control logic.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high containment (score 8) and low diffusion (score 2), indicating good architectural packaging of the DP. Other modules have lower containment and higher diffusion, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the IM boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible as the worksite model/map generated by the NGHE Solution can be validated against acceptance criteria requiring availability to planning/execution functions and inclusion of site features and operational policies.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of the FR to the NGHE Solution IM is supported by the module's scope and containment scores, with minimal diffusion risk and well-defined external interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Clarify and document the interface and data exchange protocols between NGHE Solution and Teleoperation to mitigate potential overlap and diffusion risks.
Establish monitoring and validation mechanisms for telemetry data quality and update frequency to ensure model accuracy.
Consider defining explicit boundaries or responsibilities for operational policy management between NGHE Solution and Teleoperation to reduce coupling.

decision.justification: The current FR to DP to IM mapping is logically sound and architecturally well-contained, with all gates passed and clear verification feasibility. The NGHE Solution module effectively encapsulates the DP with minimal diffusion risk, and the FR allocation is clear. Given the high safety criticality, it is prudent to avoid unnecessary complexity by further decomposing at this stage. Instead, focus should be on addressing recommended revisions to mitigate identified diffusion risks and ensure robust interfaces and data quality.

revise.why: To clarify and document interfaces and data exchange protocols between NGHE Solution and Teleoperation, and to define explicit boundaries for operational policy management to reduce coupling and diffusion risks.

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module's containment and diffusion scores accurately reflect architectural packaging quality.
Verification methods for the worksite model/map are sufficient to ensure safety and functional compliance.
open_risks: Potential overlap and coupling between NGHE Solution and Teleoperation modules.
Telemetry data quality and update frequency impacting model accuracy.
evidence_needed: Interface and data exchange protocol documentation between NGHE Solution and Teleoperation.
Monitoring and validation mechanisms for telemetry data quality and update frequency.
Defined boundaries for operational policy management responsibilities.
